feature: Dashboard Project-Centric Redesign — Single SPA with Correct Hierarchy
description: Complete redesign of hex-nexus dashboard to implement the project-centric model: Project → Agents → Worktrees → Commits, governed by ADR → WorkPlan → HexFlo workflow. Consolidates three HTML entry points into one SolidJS SPA. 6 phases, 24 tasks.
supersession_reason: Previous workplan fixed symptoms (42 individual bugs). This workplan fixes the root cause: wrong information architecture. All remaining UX bugs from the old workplan are absorbed into the appropriate phase here.

phases.name: Consolidate — Single Entry Point
phases.description: Kill index.html and chat.html. Move all functionality into the SolidJS dashboard.html (renamed to index.html). One app, one styling system, one WebSocket strategy.

gate.command: cd hex-nexus/assets && npx tsc --noEmit && npx vite build

tasks.name: Audit legacy entry points for unique features
tasks.description: Read index.html and chat.html. Catalog every feature NOT already in the SolidJS dashboard: (1) index.html: quick-action buttons, project tab-bar selector, 2x2 grid cards, (2) chat.html: full streaming chat with tool calls, file browser panel, collapsible dashboard overlay, session management. Create a migration checklist.

tasks.name: Migrate chat.html streaming chat into SolidJS ChatView
tasks.description: The SolidJS ChatView component exists but is incomplete. Port the full streaming chat from chat.html: WebSocket message handling, tool-call rendering, markdown streaming, session create/switch/delete. Ensure it uses the SpacetimeDB chat-relay module (ADR-046: no direct REST for state).

tasks.name: Remove legacy HTML files and vanilla JS scripts
tasks.description: Delete index.html (old), chat.html, and all vanilla JS scripts they reference. Rename dashboard.html → index.html. Update rust-embed config in hex-nexus if needed. Ensure axum serves the SolidJS SPA as the sole entry point at /.

tasks.name: Unify styling — purge CSS vars, Tailwind only
tasks.description: Remove all CSS custom property theming from legacy styles.css. Dashboard.css becomes the sole stylesheet. Remove !important overrides, [style*=] selectors. All colors via Tailwind theme (extend tailwind.config with hex layer colors: domain=#58a6ff, ports=#bc8cff, usecases=#3fb950, primary=#f0883e, secondary=#d29922). Remove all inline style={{}} from components (absorbs S3.1-S3.5 from old workplan).

phases.name: Project-Centric Navigation
phases.description: Redesign the sidebar and routing so Project is THE main entity. Everything else is scoped under a project. Global views (Control Plane) aggregate across projects.

gate.command: cd hex-nexus/assets && npx tsc --noEmit && npx vite build

tasks.name: Redesign Route type with project-scoped hierarchy
tasks.description: Rewrite the Route discriminated union in router.ts. New hierarchy: (1) control-plane (aggregated dashboard), (2) project/:id (project home), (3) project/:id/agents, (4) project/:id/agents/:agentId, (5) project/:id/swarms, (6) project/:id/swarms/:swarmId, (7) project/:id/swarms/:swarmId/tasks/:taskId, (8) project/:id/adrs, (9) project/:id/workplans, (10) project/:id/workplans/:wpId, (11) project/:id/health, (12) project/:id/graph, (13) project/:id/files, (14) project/:id/chat, (15) project/:id/config/:section. Update hashToRoute/routeToHash. Breadcrumbs should reflect full path (Control Plane > Project > Swarms > Swarm-42 > Task-7).

tasks.name: Redesign sidebar as project-centric nav
tasks.description: Replace current sidebar in App.tsx. New structure: (1) Top: Control Plane link (home), (2) Project list (collapsible), (3) When a project is active, expand sub-nav: Overview, Agents, Swarms, ADRs, WorkPlans, Health, Graph, Files, Chat, Config. (4) Bottom: global items that aggregate (Inference, Fleet). Use 272px width (ADR-052) with icon-mode collapse to 48px. Active project highlighted with left border accent.

tasks.name: Kill tiling pane system — replace with tab navigation
tasks.description: Remove PaneManager, PaneLeaf, panes.ts store, and all pane-related keyboard shortcuts. Replace with simple route-based tab navigation within each project page. The center area renders ONE view at a time based on the route. If users need side-by-side, they can use browser split-screen. This eliminates the competing dual-navigation system (routes vs panes).

tasks.name: Implement URL routing with deep-linking
tasks.description: Every page must have a unique URL via hash routing. Examples: #/ (control-plane), #/project/abc123 (project home), #/project/abc123/swarms/sw-42 (swarm detail), #/project/abc123/swarms/sw-42/tasks/t-7 (task detail). Browser back/forward must work. Remove localStorage-based navigation state. Keep localStorage only for user preferences (theme, collapsed sidebar).

phases.name: Project Home — The Hub Page
phases.description: Build the project home page that shows the full picture: active swarms, recent agents, health score, recent commits, and the governance→execution pipeline.

tasks.name: Build ProjectHome component with 4 quadrants
tasks.description: New ProjectHome.tsx replaces ProjectDetail.tsx. Four sections: (1) Top-left: Health ring + architecture score + violation count, (2) Top-right: Active swarms with task progress bars, (3) Bottom-left: Recent agents with status (active/stale/dead) and their worktree paths, (4) Bottom-right: Recent commits from all worktrees (via GET /api/{pid}/git/log). Each section links deeper (click swarm → swarm detail, click agent → agent detail).

tasks.name: Build governance pipeline banner
tasks.description: At the top of ProjectHome, show a horizontal pipeline: ADRs (count + latest) → WorkPlans (active + status) → Swarms (active + task completion %). Each node is clickable and shows a mini-status. Visually represents the ADR → WorkPlan → HexFlo workflow. Uses hex layer colors (ADR=orange, WorkPlan=purple, Swarm=green).

tasks.name: Build Control Plane as aggregated project view
tasks.description: Redesign ControlPlane.tsx as an aggregated view across ALL projects. Show: (1) Project cards with health badges and active swarm count, (2) Global swarm activity (tasks in-progress across all projects), (3) Inference usage summary (total tokens, cost, active providers), (4) Fleet status (online nodes, capacity). This is the landing page — its job is to route users into the right project.

phases.name: Agent → Worktree → Commit Drill-Down
phases.description: Build the project-scoped agent views that show the full chain: Agent → assigned tasks → worktree → commits.

tasks.name: Build project-scoped AgentList view
tasks.description: New AgentList.tsx (project-scoped, replaces global AgentFleet for within-project use). Queries SwarmAgent table filtered by project's active swarms (swarm.project_id === activeProjectId). Each agent card shows: name, role, status, heartbeat age, assigned tasks count, worktree path. Click → agent detail. Also show RemoteAgent entries for this project.

tasks.name: Build AgentDetail view with worktree + commits
tasks.description: New AgentDetail.tsx at route project/:id/agents/:agentId. Shows: (1) Agent info (name, role, model, status, heartbeat), (2) Assigned tasks (from SwarmTask where agent_id matches), (3) Worktree info (path, branch, from SwarmAgent.worktree_path), (4) Recent commits in that worktree (GET /api/{pid}/git/task-commits?agent_id=X or filter git/log by worktree branch). Full chain visible: Agent → Tasks → Worktree → Commits.

tasks.name: Build SwarmDetail view with task→agent mapping
tasks.description: New SwarmDetail.tsx at route project/:id/swarms/:swarmId. Shows: (1) Swarm metadata (name, topology, status, created_at), (2) Task list with status badges and assigned agent names, (3) Task DAG visualization (dependency graph if deps exist), (4) Agent roster for this swarm with health indicators. Click task → shows agent + worktree + commits inline. Click agent → AgentDetail page.

tasks.name: Build WorkPlanDetail view linked to swarm execution
tasks.description: New WorkPlanDetail.tsx at route project/:id/workplans/:wpId. Shows: (1) Workplan phases with tier numbers and gates, (2) Tasks within each phase with status + assigned agent, (3) Link to the HexFlo swarm executing this workplan (if memory key 'workplan:{wpId}:swarm_id' exists), (4) Execute/Pause/Resume buttons wired to REST endpoints. Visually connects the WorkPlan → HexFlo pipeline.

phases.name: ADR → WorkPlan → HexFlo Governance Flow
phases.description: Wire the governance pipeline so users can trace from an ADR decision through its workplan to the executing swarm and see live progress.

tasks.name: Enhance ADRBrowser with workplan links
tasks.description: In ADRBrowser.tsx, for each ADR, scan workplans for those referencing this ADR (workplan.adr field). Show a 'WorkPlans' badge with count. Click badge → navigate to the workplan. If workplan has an active swarm, show swarm status inline (green dot = active, checkmark = completed). This creates the ADR → WorkPlan link.

tasks.name: Build governance timeline view
tasks.description: New GovernanceTimeline.tsx — a vertical timeline view at project/:id showing the full governance flow: (1) ADR created (date, title, status), (2) WorkPlan created referencing ADR, (3) HexFlo Swarm initialized from workplan, (4) Tasks created → assigned → completed, (5) Final validation verdict. Each node shows timestamp, status, and is clickable to drill into the detail page. This is the 'story of how a feature was built' view.

tasks.name: Wire project config page to show live sync status
tasks.description: Enhance ConfigPage.tsx to show sync status for each config type: skills, agent definitions, hooks. For each item, show source_path (filesystem) and synced_at (when last pushed to SpacetimeDB via ADR-044 config sync). Add a 'Resync' button that triggers POST /api/config/sync for the project. Shows the connection between filesystem config and SpacetimeDB state.

phases.name: Polish — Responsive, A11y, Design System
phases.description: Final polish: responsive layout, accessibility, design system enforcement, keyboard shortcuts cleanup. Absorbs remaining items from old workplan S3-S5.

gate.command: cd hex-nexus && cargo check && cd assets && npx tsc --noEmit && npx vite build

tasks.name: Responsive layout — collapsible sidebar + mobile nav
tasks.description: Sidebar: 272px default, icon-mode collapse to 48px at md breakpoint, hidden drawer on mobile. Right panel: collapsible at lg breakpoint. Mobile: bottom tab bar with 4 items + More menu. All fixed-width panels (ADRBrowser 280px, FileTree 280px, WorkplanView 440px) become responsive with min/max constraints.

tasks.name: Accessibility pass — focus, ARIA, color contrast
tasks.description: Add focus-visible:ring-2 to all interactive elements. Add aria-label to icon-only buttons. Add text labels alongside color-only status indicators (agent status, health score). Replace emoji icons with SVG. Ensure color contrast meets WCAG AA. Add skip-to-content link.

tasks.name: Design system enforcement — hex layer colors everywhere
tasks.description: Apply ADR-052 design system consistently: (1) Dark mode default bg-[#0a0e14], (2) Hex layer colors in all architecture views (Domain=#58a6ff, Ports=#bc8cff, UseCases=#3fb950, Primary=#f0883e, Secondary=#d29922), (3) Status colors standardized (green=active, cyan=in-progress, yellow=warning, red=error, gray=idle), (4) JetBrains Mono for code/identifiers, Inter for UI. Remove all hardcoded hex colors from components.

tasks.name: Clean up keyboard shortcuts for new navigation
tasks.description: Remove pane-related shortcuts (Ctrl+\, Ctrl+-, Ctrl+W for panes, Ctrl+] Ctrl+[). Keep: Ctrl+P (command palette), Tab (Plan/Build toggle), Ctrl+N (spawn agent). Add: Ctrl+1-5 to switch project sub-pages (Overview, Agents, Swarms, ADRs, Config). Update shortcuts help dialog.

tasks.name: Rebuild hex-nexus binary and smoke test
tasks.description: Build the final SolidJS assets (npx vite build). Rebuild hex-nexus with rust-embed (cargo build -p hex-nexus --release). Start the daemon and manually verify: (1) / serves the SolidJS SPA, (2) All routes work via hash navigation, (3) SpacetimeDB subscriptions connect, (4) Project drill-down works end-to-end (Project → Agents → Worktree → Commits), (5) ADR → WorkPlan → Swarm links resolve.
